content.name: AISP Conformance Standard — Ecosystem v1.0.0

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {rule_id, severity, passed, message}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.description: Ecosystem conformance extension for AISP skills: discovery index integrity, the aisp/ directory contract, _shared/ scope, the AISP-native lifecycle (generation / evolution / distribution, tool-agnostic), the default-generated but core-optional same-folder SKILL.md sidecar bridge, and generated per-skill README projections. Loaded on top of AISP_Standard.core. Not an executable flow.
content.fractal_exempt: Standard library reference module — no executable flow graph.

params.aisp_dir: string|null, default: null, Path to the aisp/ directory to check.

ecosystem_rules.description: EC1-EC8: ecosystem and lifecycle conformance rules.

EC1.name: aisp/ Directory Contract
EC1.check: The aisp/ directory contains README.md (MUST). aisp_list.py and aisp_list.json are SHOULD (the discovery toolkit). README.md explains: what this is, how to discover, how to run, how to add a skill.
EC1.how_to_verify: Assert aisp/README.md exists (FAIL if missing). Warn if aisp_list.py or aisp_list.json is missing.

EC2.name: Index ↔ Folder Consistency
EC2.check: aisp_list.json is a cache derived from the skill folders. The folder is the single source of truth; if the index disagrees, re-running aisp_list.py --json regenerates it to match.
EC2.how_to_verify: Glob *_aisp/aisp.aisop.json and compare each id/summary/category/tags/when_to_use/risk_level against the corresponding aisp_list.json row. Warn on drift (the folder wins).

EC3.name: _shared/ Scope
EC3.check: _shared/ holds cross-skill shared resources and is NOT a skill folder (no _aisp suffix). Resources with scope:shared resolve relative to _shared/.
EC3.how_to_verify: Assert _shared/ has no aisp.aisop.json. For scope:shared resources, assert the path resolves under _shared/. Confirm the glob *_aisp/ naturally excludes _shared/.

EC4.name: Registry Provenance
EC4.check: When published, the registry records provenance (source / commit / contract_sha256 / resources_sha256) as the unit of distribution (the skill folder). Trust / scoring / scanning are generated by the registry, never trusting self-declarations.
EC4.how_to_verify: If a registry manifest is present, assert provenance hashes are recorded and computed over the skill folder. Cross-check with core M6 (no self-declared trust).

EC5.name: Generation
EC5.check: A generator builds aisp/<id>_aisp/aisp.aisop.json (lean contract + rich Tier A) + custom resources, validating id == folder name ending in _aisp, every enforced_by real, resource paths legal, and functions-referenced resources declared; it produces / updates aisp_list.json.
EC5.how_to_verify: Informative: generation is AISP-native and tool-agnostic. A generated skill MUST still satisfy core M1-M6.

EC6.name: Evolution Safety
EC6.check: An evolution MUST NOT remove a safety constraint, lower risk_level, or expand tools without justification. A change MAY edit contract fields or functions/aisop.main; changing resources enters a deliberate evolution/edit step + changelog + security scan.
EC6.how_to_verify: Across versions: assert no safety constraint was removed, risk_level was not silently lowered, and tools were not expanded without a recorded justification.

EC7.name: Default SKILL.md Sidecar Bridge
EC7.check: A native AISP skill does not require SKILL.md for core conformance. Authoring/scaffold tools and public distribution profiles SHOULD generate a thin same-folder SKILL.md sidecar bridge by default unless the author explicitly opts out. The sidecar only guides loading/running the native aisp.aisop.json (never copies logic; deletable without breaking the skill). Its name is the same-folder AISP id minus _aisp / hyphenated, lowercase, and MUST NOT contain 'anthropic' or 'claude'; description ≤ 1024 and injection-safe. Generated bridges declare metadata.generated_from_aisp: "true", metadata.aisp_program: "aisp.aisop.json", metadata.protocol: AISP V1.0.0, and SHOULD declare metadata.bridge_mode: native_sidecar.
EC7.how_to_verify: Run tools/aisp_validate.py for ecosystem warnings, tools/aisp_skill_md.py --check/--check-all for deterministic sidecar projection consistency, and tools/aisp_validate_agent_skill_bridge.py when a sidecar is expected. If SKILL.md is missing in the default profile, warn but do not fail core conformance. If SKILL.md exists: assert standalone YAML frontmatter; assert top-level frontmatter keys are limited to name, description, license, allowed-tools, and metadata; assert name derives from the same-folder AISP id; assert description is short and injection-safe; assert metadata.generated_from_aisp is true; assert metadata.aisp_program is exactly aisp.aisop.json with no absolute, remote, subdirectory, or traversal path; assert the native AISP program passes reference validation; assert the body states the thin-bridge/source-of-truth boundary and does not duplicate node logic.

EC8.name: Generated Per-Skill README
EC8.check: Each *_aisp skill SHOULD contain README.md generated from aisp.aisop.json. In strict/release profiles it is treated as MUST. The README is a bootstrap projection only; a successful check proves derivation consistency, not safety or trust. Generic non-AISOP agents can follow it only on a best-effort basis; hard guarantees require a conforming AISOP runtime. Embedded hashes are integrity hints only and never trust claims.
EC8.how_to_verify: Run tools/aisp_readme.py --check or --check-all. Normalize UTF-8 BOM and CRLF/CR to LF before comparing. Warn by default on missing/manual/drifted README, bad source marker, or unsupported generator marker; fail when strict_readme/release mode is active.

lifecycle.description: AISP's lifecycle is AISP-native and tool-agnostic; generation / evolution / distribution are handled by external tooling of the author's choice. AISP defines the package and the conformance, not the toolchain.
lifecycle.generation: Generation — produces a conformant skill folder (see EC5).
lifecycle.evolution: Evolution — change management + changelog + security scan via a deliberate evolution/edit step (see EC6).
lifecycle.distribution: Distribution — registry-agnostic; the skill folder is the unit; any registry indexes discovery + invocation + risk + provenance (see EC4).

content.summary: EC1-EC8 ecosystem / lifecycle conformance for the aisp/ directory and the AISP-native, tool-agnostic lifecycle.
content.system_prompt:
AISP ecosystem conformance standard. The skill folder is the single source of truth; the index is a cache. AISP's lifecycle (generation, evolution, distribution) is AISP-native and tool-agnostic; AISP defines the package, not the toolchain. SKILL.md is default-generated for authoring/distribution but remains core-optional and never the truth. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.
